              generic "Complete: Countermeasure CM0001 protects mission-sensitive information such as system goals, operational modes, and scientific capabilities—precisely the information threat actors gather through REC-0009. By implementing classification, access controls, and DLP on design specifications, command databases, and failure resolution documents, this countermeasure prevents unauthorized access to mission-critical data attackers would seek to obtain."               generic "Complete: Countermeasure CM0008 protects security testing reports that detail software vulnerabilities, which directly addresses how threat actors executing technique REC-0008.03 seek such vulnerability information from sources like vulnerability databases and leaked reports to exploit spacecraft systems." [e142] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "REC-0008.03->CM0008" [e143] [cursor=pointer]

            cell [e151] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Complete: Countermeasure CM0001 is relevant to technique REC-0008.04 because REC-0008.04 describes adversaries gathering sensitive mission information through business relationships like contractor networks, while CM0001 specifically directs protection of sensitive documentation and data 'at any location (ground system, contractor networks, etc.) storing design information' using access controls and encryption. This direct overlap in operational contexts shows why protecting sensitive information applies to business relationship vulnerabilities." [e152] [cursor=pointer]

              generic "Complete: Countermeasure CM0007 protects against technique REC-0008.03 by concealing software version numbers.
